decision: When config.llm_provider is None, deliver an unsummarized digest instead of erroring
reasoning: Matches PROJECT_SPEC §9 Tuần-1 milestone (digest thô, chưa có LLM); renderer-5 RF-2 tolerates any summary string; makes the tool useful at zero LLM cost
rejected: Hard-error when no LLM provider configured

decision: One run produces ONE digest aggregating ALL watched repos (not one file per repo)
reasoning: renderer-5 render() takes a flat list[SummarizedItem] and emits per-repo ## sections internally; delivery-6 deliver(str) called once; PROJECT_SPEC §4 shows one digest with multiple ## blocks
rejected: One digest file per repo

decision: Pipeline summarizes via batch summarize_items(), not per-item summarize()
reasoning: summarizer-llm-4 cross-spec constraint explicitly says pipeline wiring calls summarize_items() for graceful batch skip-log-continue
rejected: Call per-item summarize() in a loop

decision: V1 records seen items (mark_seen) but does NOT filter already-seen items out of the digest
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §6 S3 V1=ghi, V2=delta; keeps V1 idempotent-recording honest, defers real delta to V2
rejected: Filter seen items now (full delta in V1)

decision: AuthError (401/403) is fatal (exit 1), not a per-repo skip
reasoning: All repos share one GITHUB_TOKEN; an invalid token cannot succeed for any repo, so skipping per-repo would just waste the run
rejected: Skip the repo on AuthError like a 404

decision: Per-repo failure isolation catches recoverable CollectorError subclasses (404/InvalidRepoError/NetworkError) around each repo; AuthError stays fatal
reasoning: One bad repo must not kill the digest (PROJECT_SPEC §5 V1 done = no rate-limit crash); exact catch granularity to be confirmed by architect at S3
rejected: Abort whole run on any repo error

decision: All-repos-fail run exits 0 (success-with-warnings) after delivering the no-new-items doc
reasoning: render([]) returns a valid no-new-items doc that is delivered; a delivered digest = success; exit 1 reserved for fatal config/auth/delivery errors
rejected: Exit non-zero when zero items collected

decision: Terminal RateLimitError stops further collection but delivers items already collected (exit 0)
reasoning: Collector backoff already exhausted; partial digest is more useful than a crash; aligns with PROJECT_SPEC §5 no-rate-limit-crash criterion
rejected: Abort run with exit 1 on rate limit
Retry indefinitely

decision: No-LLM-provider wraps each RawItem as SummarizedItem with empty summary string, renders, exits 0
reasoning: D-1; renderer-5 RF-2 tolerates empty summary per field; keeps Tuần-1 milestone reachable at zero LLM cost
rejected: Error when no provider
Render RawItem directly bypassing SummarizedItem (renderer requires SummarizedItem)

decision: Security AC: assert neither token nor api_key substring appears in captured stderr/logs/digest via log-capture test
reasoning: RF-1 (HIGH); reuses collector-2 ADR-004 + summarizer-llm-4 ADR-008 no-secret-logging discipline
rejected: Trust upstream not to leak without an explicit pipeline-level test

decision: One run delivers exactly one digest (single deliver(content) call), never one-per-repo
reasoning: renderer aggregates all repos into one string; delivery-6 deliver(str) is single-shot; A2
rejected: One digest file per repo

decision: No-LLM path uses a visible placeholder summary '(no summary — LLM disabled)' instead of an empty string, asserted visible in rendered output
reasoning: Stakeholder requested an explicit marker so readers see summaries were intentionally skipped, not a failure; renderer omits empty summaries (AC-5-017) but emits non-empty ones verbatim, so a non-empty placeholder is required to surface the state
rejected: Empty summary string (omitted by renderer, ambiguous)
summary=None (renderer expects str)

decision: Linear run_pipeline free function with private helpers; adapters are locals, never attributes
reasoning: Keeps GITHUB_TOKEN/api_key out of any retained object (BR-7-006, RF-1); matches render() functional style
rejected: Pipeline class holding adapters as attributes (token/key on self = RF-1 risk)

decision: Derive LLM model from provider (_PROVIDER_MODEL map + fallback) and build Redis best-effort (REDIS_URL env, _NullCache on failure) INSIDE the pipeline — no Config field added (resolves GAP-001)
reasoning: SummarizerConfig.model is required + summarizer needs SummaryCache, but Config lacks both and proposal Non-Goal forbids Config redesign; AC-7-009 already presumes best-effort cache; avoids S2 return
rejected: Add llm_model+redis_url to Config (violates no-Config-redesign Non-Goal, needs S2 return)
Hardcode single model constant (wrong for ollama vs openai)

decision: Exception->action taxonomy: catch most-specific-first AuthError(fatal exit1) -> RateLimitError(break+deliver, exit0) -> InvalidRepoError/NetworkError/CollectorError(skip+continue)
reasoning: All four subclass CollectorError so catch order is load-bearing; matches AC-7-004/005/017 exactly; single most error-prone area for S4
rejected: Single except CollectorError with isinstance dispatch (fragile ordering)

decision: No-secret-logging: log only repo names/counts/our-own-exception-class; never log raw upstream exception; token->collector ctor, key->summarizer ctor only
reasoning: RF-1 HIGH; reuses collector-2 ADR-004 + summarizer-llm-4 ADR-008; log-capture test asserts no secret substring (AC-7-014, BR-7-012)
rejected: Trust upstream not to leak without pipeline-level assertion

decision: All-repos-fail AND terminal rate-limit both exit 0 when a digest was delivered; exit 1 reserved for ConfigError/AuthError/DeliveryError (resolves GAP-003)
reasoning: render([]) is a valid delivered artifact; matches analyst _decisions AC-7-006/017; confirmed at Mini-gate A; no distinct exit code added
rejected: Distinct exit code 2 = delivered-but-empty (pins exit0 in spec, would cascade to CLI contract + S2)

decision: No-LLM path wraps each RawItem as SummarizedItem(summary=NO_LLM_PLACEHOLDER='(no summary — LLM disabled)'); summarizer neither constructed nor called
reasoning: D-1 + AC-7-022: renderer omits empty summaries but emits non-empty verbatim, so a visible placeholder is required; BR-7-010
rejected: Empty-string summary (omitted by renderer, ambiguous)

decision: Implemented run_pipeline as a free function with _collect_all/_summarize private helpers; adapters constructed as locals (token/key never stored on self)
reasoning: ADR-001; RF-1 compliance; matches render() functional style
rejected: Pipeline class

decision: _build_cache() calls redis.ping() eagerly to surface connection errors at construction time, then falls back to _NullCache on any exception
reasoning: AC-7-009 best-effort degradation; eager ping gives cleaner failure semantics than lazy miss
rejected: Lazy connection (fail on first get/set)

decision: Catch AuthError then RateLimitError then (InvalidRepoError, NetworkError, CollectorError) — most-specific-first as per ADR-003
reasoning: All four subclass CollectorError; wrong order silently mis-routes; #1 regression risk
rejected: Single except CollectorError with isinstance dispatch

decision: BrokenPipeError test uses static source inspection (assert handler code present) instead of CliRunner invocation
reasoning: CliRunner mock stdout has no real fileno(); os.dup2(devnull, sys.stdout.fileno()) cannot execute in-process. Behavior is verified by delivery-6 integration tests (the handler was authored in that change and was not changed). Static inspection confirms the contract is preserved.
rejected: subprocess approach (slow, complex)
Multiple incremental mock patches (failed 3 times — fileno not patchable through Click's stream wrapping)

decision: Updated test_cli.py:test_run_valid_config_exits_zero to mock run_pipeline — the test was written against the stub and would now attempt a real GitHub call
reasoning: The test was an integration stub test; now run_pipeline is real it needs a mock. Real pipeline behavior is tested in test_pipeline.py
rejected: Leaving test to use real pipeline (would fail with AuthError on fake token)

decision: test_broken_pipe_exits_0 is static inspection only — does not execute the handler
reasoning: S4 deviation: CliRunner mock stdout has no fileno(); handler verified correct by code review (cli.py lines 18-23). [EDGE-CASE] — CliRunner platform limitation, not a logic error. Severity: Low.

decision: test_run_summary_log_emitted_on_success only asserts exit_code==0; never asserts log line was emitted (AC-7-021)
reasoning: S4 code gap: caplog not asserted in test body. [AI-DETECTABLE]. AC-7-021 verified by code review — log call at pipeline.py:222-228. Severity: Low — does not affect production behavior.

decision: test_summarizer_returns_fewer_items asserts deliver() called but never checks only survivors in digest (AC-7-018)
reasoning: S4 code gap: shallow assertion. [AI-DETECTABLE]. AC-7-018 verified by code review — render receives summarized list directly, no re-merge. Severity: Low.

decision: Fixed pre-existing lint error in tests/test_cli.py (E501 docstring too long) and applied ruff format to 5 files before release
reasoning: ruff check found 1 E501 violation introduced in S4; ruff format found 5 files needing formatting. Both must be clean before tagging a release — fixing in S6 rather than opening a new S4-fix cycle since it's a trivial style fix with no logic change.
rejected: Open S4-fix cycle for a docstring trim

decision: Version bumped from 0.1.0 to 0.7.0 in pyproject.toml to reflect 7 completed feature tickets
reasoning: Each feature ticket (1-foundation through 7-scheduler-cli) is a minor version increment (new capability, backward-compatible). No existing API broken. Starting from 0.7.0 makes the version history legible.
rejected: Bump only to 0.2.0 (arbitrary)
Keep 0.1.0 until 1.0.0 release

decision: BrokenPipeError manual smoke test performed: `uv run osspulse run | head -1` exits 0 (pipe_exit:0). Without a live GITHUB_TOKEN, the process exits 1 from ConfigError before producing stdout — no BrokenPipeError is triggered in this environment. The handler is structurally confirmed by QA code review (AC-7-013).
reasoning: The BrokenPipeError only fires when stdout is written then the pipe closes. In this environment GITHUB_TOKEN is unavailable so the tool errors before producing output. Static confirmation from QA is the best available evidence. Note this for operator: verify with a real token.
rejected: Mark as unverified and block release

decision: CHANGELOG.md created in project root covering all 7 feature tickets with version history
reasoning: Good practice for a portfolio project; keeps a human-readable record of what changed per release.
rejected: CHANGELOG as a spec artifact only
